records.ety: [Gr. <?/ fit for molding, plastic, fr. <?/ to mold, to form.]

defs: A combining form signifying developing, forming, growing; as, heteroplastic, monoplastic, polyplastic.

records.ety: [L. plasticus, Gr. <?/, fr. <?/ to form, mold: cf. F. plastique.]

defs: Having the power to give form or fashion to a mass of matter; as, the plastic hand of the Creator.
quotes: See plastic Nature working to his end.

defs: Capable of being molded, formed, or modeled, as clay or plaster; -- used also figuratively; as, the plastic mind of a child.

Pertaining or appropriate to, or characteristic of, molding or modeling; produced by, or appearing as if produced by, molding or modeling; -- said of sculpture and the kindred arts, in distinction from painting and the graphic arts.
quotes: Medallions . . . fraught with the plastic beauty and grace of the palmy days of Italian art.

defs: A substance composed predominantly of a synthetic organic high polymer capable of being cast or molded; many varieties of plastic are used to produce articles of commerce (after 1900). [MW10 gives origin of word as 1905]
